mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 52% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is above 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer spending without inflationary pressure, driving positive equity returns as corporate earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns specifically during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery periods, or the predictive IC reverses sign when the yield curve is inverted.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained labor demand and wage growth, which directly supports corporate revenue projections and produces positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01 across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290.0, elevated inflation expectations force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL stays above 290.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when real rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic strength, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP signal produces negative returns when credit spreads widen by >100bps, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales signal weakening consumer demand, which immediately pressures corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative short-term S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal reverses direction when consumer confidence indices are above 110.
